hard.owner_title: Vice President Asia Pacific Japan & ME
hard.next_step: 75% confidence 22nd Jun Coming to ProcureCon 8th 9th Jul- also Horizon- discussion with CPO on moving forward 6th Jun- CPO and eval team coming to ProcureCon SGP (8th July) want to meet 26th May- Good F2F meeting with Rohan. Project will slow due to funding caution. Is looking for a smaller way to progress- ie start with sourcing/Intake and spend- currently reviewing a new proposal to submit- Zycus clear favored vendor, though Coupa still in the mix
hard.ais_why: Buyer framed Phase 1 as P2P/AP-focused, not AI-focused (Astrid, 19 Jan 2026); Agentic AI is in product scope and Coupa is pushing AI competitively, but Cebu Pacific has not mandated AI, so AIS holds at 5 Moderate.

north_star_verdict.headline: Cebu Pacific keeps Zycus as its clear favored vendor and the relationship is live (three buyer leaders confirmed for ProcureCon 8-9 Jul and Horizon 21 Jul), but the buyer explicitly paused the RFP for funding caution and is de-scoping to a smaller Phase 1, so the 30 Jul close cannot hold and the commercial track has stalled.
north_star_verdict.math: 24 days to close (close 2026-07-30, swept 2026-07-06). Stage Formal Evaluation entered 19 Jan 2026 (qualified date), roughly 5.6 months in stage; RFI released 30 Jan 2026, RFP planned then paused 8 May 2026. No pricing, commercial proposal, contract, or InfoSec work has started in any 2026 call or activity. Buyer stated the project will slow due to funding caution (Next Step ~6 Jun 2026) and the SAP S/4 HANA cutover is Q3/Q4 next year, both sequencing a full decision well past 30 Jul. Close date was last pushed from 30 Jun to 30 Jul on 11 May 2026 with no buyer-confirmed decision date; a further multi-month slip is required.
evidence: Astrid Tanquieng emailed 8 May 2026: 'The RFP has been paused for...' confirming the evaluation is on hold (Task 08 May 2026)

Next Step ~6 Jun 2026: 'Project will slow due to funding caution. Is looking for a smaller way to progress- ie start with sourcing/Intake and spend- currently reviewing a new proposal to submit- Zycus clear favored vendor, though Coupa still in the mix'
Buyer finance speaker 19 May 2026: 'we put a lot of constraints right now from a cash flow perspective, from a Capex perspective' (Avoma follow-up 19 May 2026)
No pricing, commercial proposal, or contract mechanics appear in any 2026 call, Next Step, or activity
Rohan Kapoor, Anna Pamela Dela Pasion and Astrid Tanquieng confirmed for ProcureCon Singapore 8-9 Jul and Horizon SEA 21 Jul (Tasks 02-18 Jun 2026), showing the relationship is live despite the pause

metrics.narrative: The buyer has articulated clear operational pain but has not attached a single number to it. Astrid Tanquieng described the environment on 19 Jan 2026 as 'our process is highly manual' with 'fragmented systems' and non-PO payment requests that cannot be monitored. On 19 May 2026 the finance-side speaker framed the whole initiative through cash-flow and Capex constraints rather than a savings target. No cost-per-invoice, exception rate, cycle-time, or headcount figure has been quantified on any call, and no ROI or value workshop has run. Building a quantified Phase 1 business case (touchless invoice rate, AP FTE hours, exception reduction) is the move that unlocks finance sign-off.
sources: Next Step 13 Mar 2026 'refocus from CP on finance'

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is Mark Cezar, CFO, named as the primary Salesforce contact, and the initiative is finance-controlled: the 19 May 2026 call was driven by a finance speaker who anchored every decision to 'cash flow' and 'Capex' constraints, and the RFP pause itself is a finance decision. Mark Cezar has never appeared on a call or email in the record, so his sponsorship is entirely passive and the funding release depends on someone he has not met. Procurement (Rohan Kapoor, Anna Pamela Dela Pasion) sponsors the initiative but does not own the budget. The move is to convert Rohan into the sponsor who carries a right-sized Phase 1 business case directly to Mark Cezar and get finance to engage Zycus for the first time.
sources: Next Step 13 Mar 2026 'refocus from CP on finance'

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are partly confirmed from the 19 Jan 2026 discovery and the 21 May 2026 pain-point email. Astrid Tanquieng named the concrete asks: a portal for internal users to raise and track non-PO payment requests, automatic approvals, auto-processing of low-value/low-risk invoices from fixed-rate contracts, and automated PO matching, all while integrating with SAP rather than replacing it ('whatever system we choose for p two p should be able to integrate with that'). Change management and a user-friendly experience were flagged as factors ('as part of change management... a very user friendly system'). Astrid was explicit the formal scoring rubric was not yet firmed up ('we haven't firmed up the actual criteria'), so criteria are directional not final, and the RFP that would harden them is paused.
sources: Avoma discovery call 19 Jan 2026 (Astrid Tanquieng, Akshay)
Task 21 May 2026 'key pain points mostly in the P2P environment'

decision_process.narrative: The process the buyer is running, read from the activity sequence, is an RFI-then-RFP evaluation now stalled at the RFI stage. The RFI Letter of Invitation plus MNDA released 30 Jan 2026, a demo ran 04-05 Mar 2026, the RFP was planned for the following month, then paused on 8 May 2026 for funding caution. Procurement (Astrid, then escalating to Rohan Kapoor and Anna Pamela Dela Pasion) runs the working evaluation; finance controls whether it resumes. The July events (ProcureCon 8-9 Jul, Horizon SEA 21 Jul) are the buyer-pulled touchpoints where Rohan and team re-engage. There is no documented approval chain or mutual close plan, and the SAP S/4 HANA cutover (Q3/Q4 next year) is an explicit external gate the buyer is sequencing against.
sources: Next Step History 30 Jan / 04 Mar / 08 May 2026

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are barely started. The only paper activity is the MNDA released with the RFI on 30 Jan 2026 and a request for a revised NDA with complete details on 21 Mar 2026, both handled by Luk/Astrid. No MSA, SOW, order form, InfoSec review, or SAP S/4 integration scoping has been discussed on any call, and no procurement or legal timeline exists because the RFP is paused. Anne Tieng (VP Legal Affairs) and Katrina Reynoso (IT/compliance) are mapped as the eventual legal and InfoSec gates. Visibility will come only once a phased proposal is accepted and a mutual close plan is drafted.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Anne Tieng (VP Legal), Katrina Reynoso (IT compliance)

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is well established and owner-attributed. Astrid Tanquieng (Manager, Operations Systems and Services Contracts) detailed on 19 Jan 2026 a highly manual, fragmented AP process with no way to monitor payment requests, a need to automate non-PO payment requests and auto-process low-value/low-risk invoices, and PO-matching problems. Akshay (Zycus) reframed the root cause as upstream SAP PR/PO quality driving downstream invoice exceptions, which the buyer accepted. The pain owners are the AP and finance organizations (Hazel Navarro, Operations Accounting Manager; Agnes Alvarez, Assistant Manager AP) under CFO Mark Cezar, with procurement (Rohan Kapoor, Anna Pamela Dela Pasion) sponsoring. The pain is real and acknowledged but not urgent enough to override the funding pause.
sources: Avoma discovery call 19 Jan 2026 (Astrid Tanquieng, Akshay)
SF OpportunityContactRole: Hazel B Navarro, Agnes Alvarez

champion.narrative: Championship is developing but not yet powerful. Astrid Tanquieng is the engaged working-level owner who briefed Zycus, coordinates the process, and shared the buyer's P2P pain points, and she carries warm history as a former member of the SM Retail team that implemented Zycus ('I was part of the team that launched Zycus... very familiar with the capability', 19 Jan 2026), but she is a gatekeeper who has not pushed back on rivals or opened the CFO. What has developed is senior sponsorship: Rohan Kapoor (Group Director, Operations Procurement) called Zycus the 'clear favored vendor', is actively pursuing the relationship (requesting ProcureCon passes and F2F meetings), and is bringing his team plus Anna Pamela Dela Pasion to both ProcureCon and Horizon SEA. What is still missing is an advocate who will carry a Phase 1 business case to CFO Mark Cezar and drive urgency against the funding pause. The two July events are the moment to convert Rohan from favorable sponsor into active champion.
sources: Next Step ~6 Jun 2026 'Zycus clear favored vendor'

competition.narrative: Zycus holds the favored position but has not closed the field out. Coupa is the live named rival and remains in the mix as of ~6 Jun 2026 ('Zycus clear favored vendor, though Coupa still in the mix'), positioning AI as its differentiator ('believed to have 3 in race, Coupa pushing AI?', 10 Feb 2026). SAP Ariba sits in the Salesforce competitor field but has not surfaced in any 2026 call, so it is dormant. The strongest actual threat is not a vendor but the do-nothing/delay path driven by funding caution and the SAP S/4 sequencing. Zycus wins by formalizing the Agentic AI / Merlin wedge for AP invoice extraction and intake and by offering the phased, low-year-one commercial structure Carl floated on 19 May 2026, giving finance a way to progress within budget before Coupa re-engages.

critical_signals.text: Zycus is the buyer-stated favored vendor as of 6 Jun 2026, but Coupa is still in the mix and leading with an AI pitch, so the wedge to protect is our Agentic AI answer for AP.
The 19 May 2026 F2F with the finance lead reframed the whole project around cash-flow and Capex constraints, and the buyer asked for a smaller way to start rather than the full S2P.
The buyer now wants a right-sized Phase 1 (AP plus sourcing/intake and spend) with low year-one cost, a de-scope from the original full Source-to-Pay ambition.
Group Director Rohan Kapoor has stepped in as the senior sponsor and is bringing his team to ProcureCon and Horizon, but CFO Mark Cezar who controls the budget has still never engaged.
No price or ROI is on the table yet; the buyer paused the RFP for funding caution, so justifying a low-year-one commercial envelope is the gate to restarting.

deal_movement.summary: The deal is live at the relationship level but stalled at the commercial level. Since the 8 May 2026 RFP pause the only movement has been event logistics (ProcureCon and Horizon registrations, Jun 2026); no pricing, proposal, or decision date has landed. Close date was pushed 30 Jun to 30 Jul on 11 May 2026 and forecast lifted to Best Case, neither backed by a buyer commitment.

items.change: Three buyer leaders (Rohan Kapoor, Anna Pamela Dela Pasion, Astrid Tanquieng) confirmed for ProcureCon Singapore 8-9 Jul and Horizon SEA 21 Jul
Buyer signalled a de-scope: 'looking for a smaller way to progress - start with sourcing/Intake and spend', new proposal under review
Astrid Tanquieng confirmed the RFP has been paused for funding caution
Good F2F with the finance lead; project framed around cash-flow and Capex constraints
Close date pushed 30 Jun to 30 Jul and forecast raised Pipeline to Best Case, no buyer decision date

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the buyer-stated favored vendor as of ~6 Jun 2026, so no rival is currently ahead. The single strongest CURRENT threat is the do-nothing/delay path from funding caution and the SAP S/4 HANA cutover (Q3/Q4 next year), which paused the RFP. Coupa is the live named rival, still in the mix and pushing AI, but the buyer leans Zycus. SAP Ariba is in the Salesforce competitor field but dormant with no 2026 mention.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / Delay (funding caution + SAP S/4 sequencing)
competitors.quote: Project will slow due to funding caution. Is looking for a smaller way to progress
competitors.source: Next Step ~6 Jun 2026 + Avoma follow-up 19 May 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Offer a small, low-year-one Phase 1 (AP + intake/spend) that delivers value inside the funding constraint and paves the way for full S2P at the SAP S/4 cutover

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Zycus clear favored vendor, though Coupa still in the mix
competitors.source: Next Step ~6 Jun 2026; Next Step 10 Feb 2026 'Coupa pushing AI?'
competitors.how_we_win: Formalize the Agentic AI / Merlin wedge for AP invoice extraction and intake so the buyer's AI interest is answered by Zycus, not Coupa, before the RFP resumes

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.source: SF Competitors field (no 2026 call or Next Step mention)
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as an ERP-agnostic AP/P2P layer over SAP ECC/S4 with official adapters, avoiding a full SAP-native lock-in

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is strong on the AP automation and SAP-coexistence asks the buyer stated; the exposure is that no formal evaluation criteria are locked and no commercial/ROI case exists yet.

items.quote: our process is highly manual... they want to automate this process for automatic approvals

items.criterion: Coexist with SAP rather than replace it (integrate P2P with SAP ECC/HANA)
items.quote: whatever system we choose for p two p should be able to integrate with that
items.source: Avoma discovery call 19 Jan 2026 (Astrid Tanquieng, Akshay)

items.criterion: User-friendly experience for change management from manual to automated
items.quote: as part of change management... it would help to have a very user friendly system

items.criterion: Right-sized, low year-one commercial envelope within funding constraints
items.quote: Is looking for a smaller way to progress- ie start with sourcing/Intake and spend

items.requirement: Deliver a high-level demo featuring airline clients and the AP transformation
items.quote: what we're particularly interested to see are any airline clients that you have... how you help them transform, particularly that AP portion
items.source: Avoma discovery call 19 Jan 2026 (demo delivered 04-05 Mar 2026)

items.requirement: Submit a revised, right-sized phased proposal (AP + sourcing/intake and spend)
items.quote: looking for a smaller way to progress- ie start with sourcing/Intake and spend- currently reviewing a new proposal to submit

items.area: Economic buyer engagement (CFO Mark Cezar never on a call)

items.source: SF OpportunityContactRole Mark Cezar; Avoma 19 Jan 2026

items.quote: The RFP has been paused for...

best_practice_check.summary: The win lever is converting favored-vendor status into a funded, right-sized Phase 1 while keeping Coupa's AI pitch answered; the two July events are the highest-leverage moment.
flags: Use the ProcureCon (8-9 Jul) and Horizon (21 Jul) in-person time with Rohan Kapoor to turn him from favorable sponsor into an active champion who carries a Phase 1 case to CFO Mark Cezar.
Answer Coupa's AI positioning head-on: put the Agentic AI / Merlin AP invoice-extraction and intake story in front of the buyer before the paused RFP resumes.
Break the finance stalemate with a low-year-one, phased commercial structure sized to the cash-flow/Capex constraint, backed by a quantified AP automation ROI the CFO can approve.

items.risk: Favorable senior sponsor but passive on urgency; deferring to finance and asking for a smaller scope

items.name: Anna Pamela Mollasgo-Dela Pasion
items.title: Director Procurement
items.risk: Engaged via events and email; needs to stay looped as the phased scope is defined

items.name: Astrid Collado-Tanquieng

items.name: Anne Tieng

champion_strength.summary: Astrid Tanquieng is a developing working-level champion (engaged, coordinates the process, warm on the SM Retail Zycus history) but has not opened the CFO or defended Zycus against Coupa; senior sponsor Rohan Kapoor calls Zycus the favored vendor and is bringing his team to both July events, which is the moment to convert him into an active advocate. Missing: an advocate who carries a Phase 1 business case to CFO Mark Cezar.
champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 19 Jan 2026; Next Step ~6 Jun 2026; Tasks 02-18 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Agentic AI is in product scope and Akshay pitched AI-based invoice extraction, matching, and conversational PR/PO guidance on 19 Jan 2026, but the buyer's own framing stayed P2P/AP-first with AI as a later phase, and Coupa is the one leading with AI. The Merlin story is present but under-leveraged versus the competitive AI pressure.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 Moderate: Agentic AI is the only AI SKU in scope and there is competitive AI pressure ('Coupa pushing AI'), but the buyer framed Phase 1 as P2P/AP automation, not AI capability, and said it will 'focus on fixing the manual processing first before we even go into that' (Astrid, 19 Jan 2026). The calls corroborate the Moderate rating, not higher.

items.detail: Buyer paused the RFP for funding caution and is de-scoping to a smaller Phase 1; no funding is released without CFO sign-off
CFO Mark Cezar, the economic buyer, has never engaged on any call or email; sponsorship is entirely working-level and procurement-level
SAP S/4 HANA cutover (Q3/Q4 next year) is an external sequencing gate the buyer is timing the full decision against
Scope narrowed from full Source-to-Pay to a phased AP + intake/spend Phase 1, a defensive cost-cutting/phasing signal

confidence_signals.summary: Relationship is live and warm (buyer-pulled events, favored-vendor status), but the commercial thread has thinned since the 8 May RFP pause with no pricing or decision date. Not cooling on relationship, stalled on commercials.
items: Buyer requested and confirmed attendance at two Zycus events (ProcureCon, Horizon), a pull signal (Tasks 02-18 Jun 2026)
No pricing, proposal, or decision date has advanced since the RFP pause (8 May 2026)

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Pay (S2P) transformation across the procurement cycle
scope_change.to: Phased Phase 1: AP automation plus sourcing/intake and spend, low year-one cost, S2C later
scope_change.detail: On ~6 Jun 2026 the buyer asked for 'a smaller way to progress- ie start with sourcing/Intake and spend' after pausing the RFP for funding caution (8 May 2026); a defensive de-scope driven by cash-flow/Capex constraints and SAP S/4 sequencing, not a loss to a rival.

customer_preference.evidence: Next Step ~6 Jun 2026: 'Zycus clear favored vendor, though Coupa still in the mix'; Astrid on 19 Jan 2026 carries warm SM Retail Zycus history ('I was part of the team that launched Zycus')

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (highly manual, fragmented AP, unmonitored payment requests); no cost-per-invoice, cycle-time, exception-rate, or savings figure quantified, and finance framed the project through cash-flow/Capex constraints on 19 May 2026

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is Cebu Pacific's clear favored vendor with a rising senior sponsor in Rohan Kapoor, but the buyer paused the RFP for funding caution and de-scoped to a smaller Phase 1, so the one thing between us and progress is a funded, right-sized commercial case the CFO will approve.

win_position.text: Win holds in the mid-50s because we are the buyer-stated favored vendor (Next Step ~6 Jun 2026) with strong AP-automation and SAP-coexistence fit, but it is capped below that by an unengaged CFO and a paused, de-scoped evaluation.

Rohan Kapoor, Group Director of Operations Procurement, called Zycus the 'clear favored vendor' and is bringing his team to ProcureCon and Horizon, giving us the senior air-cover most Formal-Evaluation deals lack.
The economic buyer, CFO Mark Cezar, has never appeared on a single call or email, and it is his funding caution that paused the RFP, so the deal cannot advance until finance engages.

The scope has narrowed from full Source-to-Pay to a low-cost Phase 1 (AP + intake/spend), a defensive cost-cutting signal that both shrinks the deal and reflects the funding pressure, while Coupa stays in the mix pushing AI.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum on the commercial track has stalled since the 8 May 2026 RFP pause: no pricing, proposal, or decision date has landed in any 2026 activity.

Relationship momentum is live and buyer-pulled: three buyer leaders confirmed for ProcureCon (8-9 Jul) and Horizon SEA (21 Jul), and Rohan requested the F2F and event passes himself (Tasks 02-18 Jun 2026).
The close date was pushed 30 Jun to 30 Jul on 11 May 2026 with no buyer-confirmed decision date, and the SAP S/4 cutover (Q3/Q4 next year) is sequencing the full decision well past July.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer is actively reviewing a new phased Zycus proposal and invited Zycus to keep engaging at two of its own events, showing genuine intent to continue with us.
Commitment is procurement-level only: no CFO engagement, no signed criteria, and the RFP that would formalize commitment is paused, so there is no contractual or budget commitment yet.

deal_risk.text: The primary risk is inertia, not a rival: funding caution and the SAP S/4 cutover could let this drift into no-decision even though Zycus is favored.
Coupa remains in the mix leading with an AI pitch, so a prolonged pause gives it time to re-engage and reframe the evaluation around AI where we are currently under-positioned.

customer_preference.evidence: Zycus clear favored vendor (Next Step ~6 Jun 2026)

economic_buyer_engagement.evidence: CFO Mark Cezar never engaged; finance paused the RFP

scope_trend.evidence: De-scoped from full S2P to a low-cost Phase 1 (Next Step ~6 Jun 2026)

items.trigger: Buyer 'looking for a smaller way to progress' and reviewing a new proposal
items.expected_effect: Gives finance a way to progress inside the cash-flow/Capex constraint and unblocks the funding pause.

items.action: Build a quantified AP automation ROI (touchless rate, AP hours, exceptions) for the CFO.
items.trigger: Finance framed the project around cash-flow and Capex constraints with no ROI on the table
items.expected_effect: Arms Rohan with the numbers Mark Cezar needs to release funding and defends against a do-nothing outcome.

items.action: Secure a direct introduction to CFO Mark Cezar via Rohan at Horizon SEA.
items.trigger: Economic buyer has never engaged and controls the paused budget
items.expected_effect: Engages the economic buyer for the first time, the single missing link to a funded decision.

items.action: Put the Agentic AI / Merlin AP invoice-extraction story in front of the eval team.
items.trigger: Coupa still in the mix and 'pushing AI' as its differentiator
items.expected_effect: Answers the buyer's AI interest with Zycus before Coupa re-engages during the pause.
